title: Telemetry Payload
description: Wire contract for a single sensor reading published to the iPulse MQTT broker. One message = one reading from one sensor stream, plus the stream's current connection status. This shape is derived from the fields the UI displays (see device-pulse-design-system.html) and is the single source of truth for device identity, display name, sensor type, value, and status — the MQTT topic is used only for subscription routing, not for parsing identity.

deviceId.description: Stable key grouping one or more sensor streams under a physical device. A device that exposes multiple sensors (e.g. temperature and pressure) publishes separate messages with the same deviceId but different sensorType values. Must be non-empty.

name.description: Human-readable display name shown in dashboard rows and the detail screen. May be shared across sensor streams of the same device. Must be non-empty.

sensorType.description: Sensor channel this reading belongs to. Matched case-insensitively by the consumer.

value.description: The measured reading. Numeric only — never a string.

unit.description: Optional unit override. When omitted the consumer derives the unit from sensorType: temperature → °C, pressure → hPa, humidity → %RH.

timestamp.description: Optional epoch milliseconds at which the reading was taken. When omitted the consumer stamps the message with its receive time.

status.description: Connection status of the sensor stream at the moment of publishing. Drives the per-device connection chip in the UI: live → cyan 'live', reconnecting → amber 'reconnecting', offline → dim 'offline'.
